Author: AemiliusXIV
Name: Orchestrion Aria
Punchline: BGM replacement with local audio support
Description:
Play and replace FFXIV background music. Supports local MP3/WAV files from your PC alongside all in-game tracks, with fade transitions and volume sync.

Originally by perchbird & Meli: https://github.com/perchbirdd/OrchestrionPlugin
Changelog:
Fixed a crash when playing a random song from a playlist that mixes your local
tracks with game BGM. Picking random from a mixed playlist no longer throws.

The "delete original after importing" option now only shows when imports are set
to copy into the plugin's storage. If you import a file by reference instead, that
original is your only copy, so the option stays hidden and cannot remove it.

Author: AemiliusXIV
Name: Party Sorter
Punchline: Drag party members to reorder your party list.
Description: Hold Ctrl (configurable) and drag a party member onto another to reorder. Saves each group's preferred order and automatically restores it when the same members meet again — perfect for statics that want a consistent layout every pull. Supports per-group auto-apply modes, locking saved orders against accidental overwrites, and per-duty separate orders. Commands: /psorter (open settings), /psorter save (save current order to chat).

Author: AemiliusXIV
Name: ChronoLog
Punchline: OBS chapter markers and YouTube timestamps for every raid pull. Use /chrono.
Description:
Tracks pulls, wipes and clears in any duty. Per pull: fight name, phase, boss HP%, first death, duration.

Outputs OBS chapter markers via websocket, a paste-ready YouTube description block, and a direct push to a live stream's description over the YouTube Data API.

Short resets are filtered out automatically. /chrono to open, /chrono cfg for settings.
Changelog: Two small fixes. The floating mark button now draws its star icon properly instead of garbled characters. And a mark placed on a pull that has already finished now survives a reload, where before it could quietly disappear.

Author: AemiliusXIV
Name: Quiet Tips
Punchline: Hide tooltips when you don't want them, keep them when you do.
Description: Stops action, item, pop-up help and crossbar hint tooltips from cluttering the screen. Toggle each type independently for in-combat and out-of-combat, set a duty override (follow combat rules, always show, or always hide while in an instance), and keep an exception so tooltips come back automatically whenever an inventory, armoury or character window is open. Original game settings are restored when the plugin is disabled. Commands: /quiettips (open settings), /quiettips toggle|on|off.
Changelog: Tooltips now hide again after closing inventory or character windows - they could get stuck showing in some cases before. When toggling the plugin on or off, you'll see a toast notification rather than a chat message by default. New option in settings: show a small icon in the server info bar that lets you toggle the plugin with a click.

Author: AemiliusXIV
Name: Clarion
Punchline: Save and apply Party Finder recruitment presets instantly.
Description:
Store a full Party Finder recruitment setup (duty, job slots, description, objective, loot rule, one player per job, language, and more) as a named preset and apply it in one go, instead of filling the form out by hand every time.

Fill a preset and review it, or fill and post the party in a single click. Optional auto-refresh keeps an active listing alive near the end of its hour, with the windows closed, and leaves your away status alone.

Open the Recruitment Criteria form (Recruit Members) to save or apply. Use /clarion.
Changelog: Applying a preset now opens Party Finder and the recruitment form itself if they're closed, so you can post a party in one click from nothing. A compact quick pop-up appears beside the recruitment form for fast applying; you can move it or turn it off in settings. Hover the (?) on any preset row to see exactly what it would set.
